title: Evangelion WTF S I am confused
display_title: Evangelion WTF S I am confused
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion WTF :S I am confused             Quote Post by flyingeagle666 » Tue Jan 11, 2011 7:21 pm So I am pretty new to Evangelion First I watched Evangelion 1.0 and 2.0 I then came on this site and was advised to watch the Neon Genesis series. I have now completed it. I was enjoying it until the last 2-3 episodes and now I am like WTF just happened? lol I dont get it at all, it was an aweful ending imo. Seriously I dont understand what I just watched Can someone explain things to me lol

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Tue Jan 11, 2011 7:32 pm ...

[]: Re: Evangelion WTF :S I am confused             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Jan 11, 2011 7:36 pm flyingeagle666 wrote: I dont get it at all, it was an aweful ending imo. Seriously I dont understand what I just watched

[]:             Quote Post by flyingeagle666 » Tue Jan 11, 2011 7:54 pm thanks guys Looks like I need to watch Death and Rebith and also End of Evangelion I have to say that I am enjoying Rebuild a lot more than the anime itself, not that the anime is bad btw (except ending is confusing lol)

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Jan 11, 2011 8:05 pm flyingeagle666 wrote: Death and Rebith

[]: Re: Evangelion WTF :S I am confused             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Tue Jan 11, 2011 11:59 pm flyingeagle666 wrote: Can someone explain things to me lol

[]:             Quote Post by Galaxy News Reidio » Wed Jan 12, 2011 6:35 am You know that feeling of "What the fuck"? Get used to it. It's a crucial part of Eva fandom.

[]:             Quote Post by liquidus118 » Wed Jan 12, 2011 8:33 am Mr. Tines wrote: That's a clip show plus the first 30 minutes of EoE ending on a cliffhanger; not strictly needed.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Wed Jan 12, 2011 9:47 pm There aren't director's cuts for 25 and 26. Only 21-24 have director's cuts, and 24's isn't really necessary. Before you watch EoE, you should probably be aware of the concurrency debate. It's split into two distinct camps; no concurrency and strong concurrency. I think there's also a weak concurrency arguement, but no one cares about it. No concurrency: Some people prefer not to think to much about it, so they don't. They believe that the endings are separate. Strong concurrency: There are also people who prefer to have a detailed unmangled continuity, to the point of obsession. They believe the endings are the same. The only thing the camps have in common is that they both have a very nasty habit of finding things that aren't really there. The majority of this forum supports strong concurrency, but there are several notable heretics, such as Nemz and Mr. Tines. There are also heretics that aren't notable, because no one likes them, such as myself. Enjoy your new cancerous confusion.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Wed Jan 12, 2011 10:39 pm TehDonutKing wrote: The only thing the camps have in common is that they both have a very nasty habit of finding things that aren't really there.

[]:             Quote Post by Number 44 » Wed Jan 12, 2011 10:40 pm TehDonutKing wrote: There aren't director's cuts for 25 and 26. Only 21-24 have director's cuts, and 24's isn't really necessary.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jan 13, 2011 12:16 am TehDonutKing wrote: 24's isn't really necessary.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Thu Jan 13, 2011 12:22 am It adds some dumb Asuka scene, a little bit of FAR bullshit, the plot hole-creating Seele scene, more Seele blahness, and a preview for a dumb movie. Clearly not worth watching.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Thu Jan 13, 2011 2:05 am Number 44 wrote: I personally can't stand Kaworu's original dub voice!

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Thu Jan 13, 2011 2:07 am Uh... Episodes 25/26 are just an alternate/concurrent version of instrumentality presented in End of Evangelion. There is nothing confusing about that.

[]:             Quote Post by flyingeagle666 » Thu Jan 13, 2011 2:31 pm Lol I'm not sure if I am less or more confused guys ;) lol Okay So I watch in order after completing 1-26 of neon genesis director's cut 21-24 death rebirth end of evangelion is this right then lol??

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Thu Jan 13, 2011 2:32 pm Close, but don't bother with Death and Rebirth; there's effectively nothing in it that isn't also in the director's cut episodes and End of Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Thu Jan 13, 2011 2:33 pm flyingeagle666 wrote: Lol I'm not sure if I am less or more confused guys ;) lol Okay So I watch in order after completing 1-26 of neon genesis director's cut 21-24 death rebirth end of evangelion is this right then lol??

[]:             Quote Post by Galaxy News Reidio » Thu Jan 13, 2011 3:01 pm And after that, you can move onto Rebuild, and jizz in your pants every time there's a closeup of a hand, or a certain piece of background music is used.

[]:             Quote Post by flyingeagle666 » Thu Jan 13, 2011 4:45 pm Galaxy News Reidio wrote: And after that, you can move onto Rebuild, and jizz in your pants every time there's a closeup of a hand, or a certain piece of background music is used.
